title: lineardate
text: lineardate Uniface data type that holds an integer representing a number of days. lineardate ParamName : Direction lineardate VariableName Use Use in params and variables blocks to define the data type of a parameter or variable. The data type can be selected when defining fields, global variables, and component variables. Description A lineardate can have a value from 0 (days) through 3652425 (10,000 years, that is, 3,652,425 days). Only a whole number of days is allowed; that is, you cannot specify hours, minutes and so on. params date pContractDate : IN date pExecutionDate : IN lineardate pWaitPeriod : OUT endparams pWaitPeriod = pExecutionDate - pContractDate Copy Related Links Data Handling in ProcScript The ProcScript language enables you to convert data from one type to another, perform arithmetic operations, extract data values, and perform string substitution. Uniface carries out implicit type conversion whenever necessary, for example, when assignments are made between non-matching data types or when comparing non-matching data types. The ProcScript language also provides a number of functions that allow you to convert from one data type to another. Date and Time Data Uniface offers a wide variety of data types for date and time values. Packing Codes for Date and Time Data Uniface data types Date, Time, and Datetime (and linear variations of these) are associated with a variety of packing codes. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
